title: Smoky German Cauliflower Casserole
description: A light, festive German casserole featuring smoky paprika-smoothed cauliflower topped with a creamy liquid cheese blend, perfect for low-carb gatherings.

ingredients.name: Shredded aged German cheese (Emmental or Gouda)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190 °C). Lightly grease a 9‑inch square baking dish with olive oil.
Steam cauliflower florets in a steamer basket over boiling water for 6–8 minutes until just tender; drain and set aside.

While the cauliflower steams, melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the heavy cream, shredded German cheese, smoked paprika, ground mustard, salt, and pepper. Stir until the cheese is fully melted and the sauce is smooth.
Fold the cooked cauliflower into the cheese sauce until well coated. Stir in grated Parmesan and chopped chives.
Transfer the cauliflower mixture into the prepared baking dish, smoothing the top with a spatula. Bake for 20–25 minutes, or until the top is lightly golden and the casserole is bubbling.
Remove from oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with extra chives if desired.
Serve hot as a striking side or light main dish.
